# terrible_f5networks_f5_modules_bigip_message_routing_router (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Specifies the name of the router profile.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) The user-defined description of the router profile.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_client_port` (Boolean) When `true`, the remote port on clientside connections (connections where the peer connected to the BIG-IP) is ignored when searching for an existing connection.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `inherited_traffic_group` (Boolean) When set to `true`, the `traffic_group` is inherited from the containing folder. When not specified, the system sets this to `false` when creating new router profile.
- `max_pending_bytes` (Number) The maximum number of bytes worth of pending messages that will be held while waiting for a connection to a peer to be created. Once reached, any additional messages to the peer will be flagged as undeliverable and returned to the originator. The accepted range is between 0 and 4294967295 inclusive.
- `max_pending_messages` (Number) The maximum number of pending messages that will be held while waiting for a connection to a peer to be created. Once reached, any additional messages to the peer will be flagged as undeliverable and returned to the originator. The accepted range is between 0 and 65535 inclusive.
- `max_retries` (Number) Sets the maximum number of time a message may be resubmitted for rerouting by the `MR::retry` iRule command. The accepted range is between 0 and 4294967295 inclusive.
- `mirror` (Boolean) Enables or disables state mirroring. State mirroring can be used to maintain the same state information in the standby unit that is in the active unit.
- `mirrored_msg_sweeper_interval` (Number) Specifies the maximum time in milliseconds that a message will be held on the standby device as it waits for the active device to route the message. Messages on the standby device held for longer than the configurable sweeper interval, will be dropped. The acceptable range is between 0 and 4294967295 inclusive.
- `parent` (String) The parent template of this router profile. Once this value has been set, it cannot be changed. The default values are set by the system if not specified and they correspond to the router type created, for example, `/Common/messagerouter` for `generic` `type` and so on.
- `partition` (String) Device partition to create router profile on.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `routes` (String) Specifies a list of static routes for the router instance to use. The route must be on the same partition as router profile.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) When `present`, ensures the router profile exists. When `absent`, ensures the router profile is removed.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `traffic_group` (String) Specifies the traffic-group of the router profile. Setting the `traffic_group` to an empty string value `""` will cause the device to inherit from containing folder, which means the value of `inherited_traffic_group` on device will be `true`.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `type` (String) Parameter used to specify the type of the router profile to manage. Default setting is `generic` with more options coming.
- `use_local_connection` (Boolean) If `true`, the router will route a message to an existing connection on the same TMM as the message was received.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
